title: Grünfeld Defense: Exchange Variation
summary: Enter the Grünfeld Exchange, where White challenges Black's center directly with pawns.
description: The Grünfeld Defense is one of Black's most dynamic openings. The Exchange Variation (4.cxd5 Nxd5 5.e4) is White's main line, directly challenging Black's central structure. This tutorial covers the main line and typical patterns.

variations.modern: 6.axb3 - The Russian Variation, avoiding doubled pawns
variations.avoidance: 4.Nf3 - Avoiding the Exchange with the Fianchetto

objectives: Understand the Grünfeld structure: central pawn vs. piece activity
Learn why White accepts doubled pawns: e4 break and space advantage
Master typical plans: e5/e6 for White, ...c5 for Black
plans: White's plan: Use the e4 break to open the position for the bishops
Black's plan: Play ...c5, ...Qc7, and create counterplay
The move ...c5 is Black's main break to challenge the center
White should avoid exchanging too much - keep the pressure
commonMistakes: Do not think the Exchange is boring - it's very tactical
Avoid playing e4 too early without proper development
Do not underestimate Black's counterattack - the Grünfeld is dangerous
White should maintain central pressure before pushing too far
completionSummary: The Grünfeld Exchange is a sharp line. You've learned the main ideas and can play both sides.

steps.title: Claim central space
steps.instruction: Play d4 to control the center.
steps.coaching: The Grünfeld is one of Black's most dynamic defenses.
steps.hint: Push the queen pawn two squares.

steps.title: Black develops the knight
steps.coaching: Black attacks d4 and prepares the Grünfeld.

steps.title: Challenge the center
steps.coaching: This is the standard setup against the Grünfeld.

steps.title: Black plays g6
steps.instruction: Black plays g6, setting up the fianchetto.
steps.coaching: This is the Grünfeld! Black will develop the bishop to g7.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nc3, developing toward the center.
steps.coaching: White develops the knight, controlling d5.

steps.title: Black plays d5
steps.instruction: Black plays d5, challenging the center.
steps.coaching: This is the key move! Black attacks c4 and controls e4.

steps.title: The Exchange begins
steps.instruction: Capture on d5 with the c-pawn.
steps.coaching: This is the Exchange Variation! White accepts the pawn structure.
steps.hint: Capture on d5 with the c-pawn.

steps.title: Black recaptures
steps.instruction: Black captures on d5 with the knight.
steps.coaching: Black gets the pawn back and simplifies the position.

steps.title: The central break
steps.instruction: Play e4 to challenge the center.
steps.coaching: This is the main line! White attacks with e4.

steps.title: Black captures
steps.coaching: Black exchanges, giving White doubled pawns.

steps.title: White recaptures
steps.coaching: White accepts the doubled pawns but gains central control.
steps.hint: Capture on c3 with the c-pawn.

steps.title: Black develops the bishop
steps.coaching: Black develops the bishop, controlling the long diagonal.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.coaching: White develops the knight, supporting e4.

steps.title: Black castles
steps.coaching: Black gets the king to safety.

steps.title: Develop the bishop
steps.coaching: White develops the bishop, completing development.

steps.title: Black's central break
steps.instruction: Black pushes c5, challenging the center.
steps.coaching: This is Black's typical break in the Grünfeld Exchange.
